_doc: Machine-readable release baselines for the M5 milestone (characterization methodology redesign + conditional pre-emphasis + BRR noise-floor strategy spike). Scaffolded at M5.0 with prelude contract entries; M5.1+ populates with measurement and conditional encoder-quality baselines as the research-spike sub-passes land. Inherits M4 baselines from baselines/m4.json (which inherits M3 from baselines/m3.json, which inherits M2 from baselines/m2.json). Three classifications: identity_gated (any drift = regression; ships with include_str! + serde-parse + assert_eq! test per M2.8.1 rule), behavior_gated (numeric or policy contracts), documentary_snapshot (informational; expected to shift on declared milestones). Identity_gated is intentionally empty at M5.0; the M5.0 default per consultant M5 plan #10 is to hold the §16.9 line (no new identity baselines unless the §16.9.1 amendment procedure activates).

behavior_gated.name: M5_NATIVE_RATE_CHARACTERIZATION_PITCH_REGISTER
behavior_gated.rule: M5 native-rate characterization MUST configure each characterization SPC such that the DSP pitch register for the test voice equals exactly 0x1000 (4096). This establishes the unity-pitch controlled comparison surface for the M5 investigation of the M4.2 shape divergence. Per SPEC §10.11. NOTE: M5.1 preflight verified that pitch_register == 0x1000 was already the case for atom-sequence voices via M2.7's core::voice_setup (which hardcodes source_sample_rate_hz = 32000); the earlier 'fractional-stepping' diagnosis of the M4.2 shape divergence is retracted (see SPEC §10.11 motivation, M5.1 update). Residual divergence is now investigated against unity-pitch baseline at M5.2 (candidates: gaussian-kernel non-impulse response at unity pitch; BRR predictor/loop-state). The M5.0 fixture-pin test asserts the locked value at compile time via include_str!; M5.1 adds the runtime-behavior test pitch_register_equals_4096_for_native_rate_signals that exercises actual harness output.

behavior_gated.name: M5_METHODOLOGY_REPAIR_BUDGET
behavior_gated.rule: M5.1 native-rate harness + M5.2 characterization re-run + at most one M5.2.1 correction iteration. Stricter than M4's 2-loop budget per consultant M5 plan #4. If methodology_unresolved persists after one correction, defer pre-emphasis permanently to M6+ and proceed to M5.4 without preset. Per SPEC §24.1.1.

behavior_gated.name: M5_RELIABLE_ALIGNMENT_THRESHOLD_INHERITED
behavior_gated.rule: M5 inherits M4.0's four-criterion reliable-alignment predicate unchanged: zcr_ratio in [0.9, 1.1]; normalized_correlation >= 0.90; alignment_best_offset < alignment_search_limit; peak_abs_error_after_gain_normalization <= 80% of unnormalized peak. No relaxation in M5.0; relaxation at M5.2 requires explicit PM review. Per SPEC §10.9 M5 threshold reaffirmation.

behavior_gated.name: M5_PRE_EMPHASIS_FILTER_FORM_CONSTRAINT
behavior_gated.rule: If M5.3 implements pre-emphasis presets (conditional on M5.2 reliable_preset_eval outcome), the filter form must be hand-derivable: FIR up to 3 taps OR one-pole IIR shelf. NO filter-design crate dependency unless explicit PM approval at M5.3 brief time. Coefficients literal-pinned in RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5-rc.md if M5.3 ships production presets. Per consultant M5 plan #13.

behavior_gated.name: M5_ATOM_PCM_STABILITY_HELD
behavior_gated.rule: M5 default position: hold the SPEC §16.9 line. Source-domain preprocessing defers to M6+ unless M5 measured data demands it AND PM authorizes a §16.9 amendment per the procedure in SPEC §16.9.1. Per consultant M5 plan #10, #19. M5.0 forward-visibility documentation only; activation requires dedicated sub-pass authorization.

behavior_gated.name: M5_RUNTIME_BUDGET
behavior_gated.rule: Native-rate characterization runtime budget (release build, 9-signal canonical set): target < 5 s, warning at > 10 s. Per consultant M5 plan #22. Block release only if CI affected — informational budget at M5.0 / M5.1 development time; m5-acceptance bundle (M5.6) may promote to a hard gate after empirical data settles.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_BEST_OFFSET_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Alignment offset (samples) at which the cross-correlation between aligned raw BRR decode and aligned mono oracle render is maximal, searched within max_offset = signal.atom.cycle_len_samples (M4.1). Numerically identical to M4.2 since pitch register was already 0x1000 then (verified at M5.1 preflight + the harness_meta block in this report). Documentary; shifts only if alignment algorithm or comparison surface changes.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_NORMALIZED_CORRELATION_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Pearson correlation between aligned raw BRR decode and aligned mono oracle render. SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment threshold = 0.90. Documentary; M5.2 confirms M4.2 values byte-identical under verified harness_meta.pitch_register == 0x1000.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ZCR_RATIO_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: zcr_oracle / zcr_raw. SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment sanity band = [0.9, 1.1]. The ~2x doubling on low/mid signals previously attributed to fractional pitch stepping (M4.2) is retracted at M5.1 (commit 900f6ad); the remaining candidate is gaussian 4-tap kernel non-impulse response at unity pitch (SPEC section 10.11 motivation, M5.1 update).

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_GAIN_DELTA_DB_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: 20*log10(oracle_rms / raw_rms) over the full window (pre-alignment). Documentary.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_GAIN_DELTA_DB_ALIGNED_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: 20*log10(aligned_oracle_rms / aligned_raw_rms) over the alignment window. Documentary.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_PEAK_ABS_ERROR_ORACLE_VS_RAW_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Max-abs delta between aligned oracle and raw, unnormalized. Documentary.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_PEAK_ABS_ERROR_AFTER_GAIN_NORMALIZATION_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Max-abs delta after rescaling oracle to match raw RMS. If close to peak_abs_error_oracle_vs_raw -> shape divergence (not gain divergence). M4.2 / M5.2 finding: close-to-equal across all 9 signals; the residual divergence is waveform-shape between raw decode (1:1 tile) and oracle (DSP playback at unity pitch through 4-tap gaussian).

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_VALIDITY_ALL_PASS_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: AND of the four SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment criteria for this signal. M5.2 finding: 0/9 signals all-pass; criterion 4 (gain_separator_ok) fails universally. Numerically identical to M4.2.

M4.2 / M5.2 finding: close-to-equal across all 9 signals; the residual divergence is waveform-shape between raw decode (1:1 tile) and oracle (DSP playback at unity pitch through 4-tap gaussian).

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_VALIDITY_ALL_PASS_ALL_8_PARTIALS_MAX_AMP_HARMONICS_1_TO_8
documentary_snapshot._note: AND of the four SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment criteria for this signal. M5.2 finding: 0/9 signals all-pass; criterion 4 (gain_separator_ok) fails universally. Numerically identical to M4.2.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_BEST_OFFSET_NORMALIZE_FALSE_MULTI_PARTIAL_CLAMP_SAFETY
documentary_snapshot._note: Alignment offset (samples) at which the cross-correlation between aligned raw BRR decode and aligned mono oracle render is maximal, searched within max_offset = signal.atom.cycle_len_samples (M4.1). Numerically identical to M4.2 since pitch register was already 0x1000 then (verified at M5.1 preflight + the harness_meta block in this report). Documentary; shifts only if alignment algorithm or comparison surface changes.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_NORMALIZED_CORRELATION_NORMALIZE_FALSE_MULTI_PARTIAL_CLAMP_SAFETY
documentary_snapshot._note: Pearson correlation between aligned raw BRR decode and aligned mono oracle render. SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment threshold = 0.90. Documentary; M5.2 confirms M4.2 values byte-identical under verified harness_meta.pitch_register == 0x1000.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_GAUSSIAN_ZCR_RATIO_NORMALIZE_FALSE_MULTI_PARTIAL_CLAMP_SAFETY
documentary_snapshot._note: zcr_oracle / zcr_raw. SPEC section 10.9 reliable-alignment sanity band = [0.9, 1.1]. The ~2x doubling on low/mid signals previously attributed to fractional pitch stepping (M4.2) is retracted at M5.1 (commit 900f6ad); the remaining candidate is gaussian 4-tap kernel non-impulse response at unity pitch (SPEC section 10.11 motivation, M5.1 update).

documentary_snapshot._note: 20*log10(oracle_rms / raw_rms) over the full window (pre-alignment). Documentary.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_CHARACTERIZATION_SUMMARY

value._inferential_closure_source: M5.1 STATUS Phase E three-way-comparison-deferred section

documentary_snapshot._note: M5.2 characterization re-run produced metrics numerically identical to M5.1 Phase E (which was byte-identical to M4.2). The unity-pitch contract (pitch_register == 0x1000) is verified active via the harness_meta block above. SPEC section 10.9 four-criterion reliable-alignment predicate: 0/7 anchor signals satisfy all four (criterion 4 gain_separator_ok fails universally; criteria 1 and 2 pass only for harmonic_16_cycle_64). Decision: methodology_unresolved per SPEC section 10.11. M5.3 pre-emphasis evaluation SKIPPED; defers permanently to M6+ unless a future milestone introduces fundamentally different characterization methodology. Next: M5.4 BRR noise-floor strategy spike.

documentary_snapshot.name: M5_2_RESIDUAL_DIVERGENCE_HYPOTHESIS
documentary_snapshot._note: M5.2 Phase E adopted Path 1 (inferential closure) per the M5.1 STATUS three-way-comparison-deferred argument: every M3.5 canonical atom sets render.force_filter_0_loop_entry = true (core::characterize_gaussian::atom_base line 69). Filter 0 BRR blocks contain no prior-sample predictor term, so the BRR decoder state is reset at every loop boundary. Within a cycle iteration the decoder state evolves through filters 0-3; at the loop wrap, filter 0 on block 0 zeroes that state again. The PCM output of cycle N+1 is therefore byte-identical to cycle N. Consequence: a three-way comparison of (1) host raw decode tiled (no state-carry) vs (2) host raw decode with predictor-state-carry across loop iterations vs (3) snes_spc oracle render reduces to a two-way comparison, because (1) and (2) produce identical PCM for these atoms. The remaining candidate cause for the (1)/(2)-vs-(3) divergence narrows to the S-DSP gaussian 4-tap kernel non-impulse response at unity pitch -- even at pitch_register = 0x1000 (step = 1.0 exactly), the DSP's gaussian kernel applies non-impulse weights at integer offsets. This hypothesis is locked as the M5.2 investigation outcome; empirical confirmation via implementation of the state-carrying decode helper is deferred to M6+ as ~30-line work whose result is predictable from the algebraic argument. M5.2.1 correction budget NOT burned -- no clear-cause implementation fix exists (the methodology limitation is structural under the current comparison-surface design). Mechanism correction history: M4.2 Phase C originally diagnosed fractional pitch stepping; SPEC section 10.11 motivation (M5.1 commit 900f6ad) retracted that diagnosis after preflight verified pitch_register was already 0x1000 since M2.7 via core::voice_setup.
